addDevice.title: Add another device
addDevice.subtitle: Use the same identity on a second device. The transfer is sealed end-to-end — no server can read it.

listen.emptyTitle: Nothing is asking to be linked yet.
listen.emptyBody: On your new device, open Understoria and choose "Bring my identity over." It will show up here by itself — keep this page open.
listen.multiWarning: More than one device is asking. Approve ONLY the one whose emoji match the screen in front of you. If none match, don't approve any of them.
listen.requestTitle: A device is asking to be linked
listen.requestBody: Linking hands that device your full identity — it will act as you. Check the emoji above match the screen on your new device.
listen.staleHint: if your app shows different emoji, tap 'ask again' there and use the newest
listen.safetyLine: Only link a device you're holding. Not trying to link anything right now? Don't tap — someone on your network could be trying their luck.
listen.badgeAriaLabel: Recognition emoji for this link request
listen.noNode: This device can't reach your community node, so tap-to-link isn't available right now. The QR method works without any server.
listen.whatMoves: What moves to the new device (and what doesn't)
listen.vpnHint: Still nothing? A VPN or iCloud Private Relay can make your two apps look like they're on different networks, so the ask never shows up here. Pause it for a minute and have the new device ask again — or use "Other ways to link" below.

otherWays.title: Other ways to link
otherWays.wordsTitle: Linking a device that's somewhere else?
otherWays.wordsBody: Get a 6-word code to read out loud — whoever holds the new device types it there. Needs your community node.
otherWays.qrTitle: No community server, or don't want it involved?
otherWays.qrBody: Show a QR the new device scans. Nothing is stored anywhere — the two devices must be in the same room.

sent.title: Sent.
sent.body: Flip back to the app — it signs in by itself. You can leave this page.

comparison.transfersTitle: What follows you to the new device

transfers.identityDetail: Both devices sign as the same member.
transfers.balanceDetail: Visible on both devices — these live in the community ledger, not on your device.
transfers.connectionDetail: The new device learns which community server to sync with and fills in posts, projects, events, and members right away.

stays.dmsDetail: DMs are end-to-end encrypted to this device. The new device starts fresh.
stays.draftsDetail: Local-only on purpose. Finish or copy anything you don't want to lose first.
stays.themeDensityDetail: Per-device on purpose — your eyes, not your identity.
stays.achievementsDetail: These rebuild on the new device once it syncs with the community.

gate.title: Heads up: this QR is your identity for 5 minutes.
gate.body1: Security cameras, doorbell cams, and webcams can read QR codes from across a room. Once it's on screen, anyone in camera view can save it and replay it within the next 5 minutes — even after you close this screen.
gate.body2: With the passphrase, that gives them everything: your name, your history, the ability to sign as you.
gate.commitment: Do this in a room you control. If you're not sure, cancel.

display.spokenInstructions: Read these six words aloud, or type them on the new device. The order matters. Don't paste — clipboard apps remember things.
display.copyHatchToggle: Can't scan — setting up the app on this same phone, or a computer? Copy the code instead
display.copyHatchStep1: Write the 6 words above on paper FIRST. If you leave this screen and it reloads, a new code is generated and the old words stop working.
display.copyHatchStep3: Open the app (or the other device's capture screen) and paste. Then type the 6 words from your paper.
display.copyHatchWarning: Copying puts the pairing code in your clipboard, which may sync across your devices or be kept in a clipboard manager's history. The code only works for the next few minutes and is useless without the six words — say them aloud, type them, or keep them on paper; never send them alongside the code. We'll try to clear the clipboard when the timer runs out.
display.copied: Copied. On the new device, choose paste on the capture screen.
display.copyFailed: Couldn't copy — this browser blocked clipboard access. Tap the code below, Select All, then Copy.
display.copySelectLabel: If the button doesn't work: tap the code below, Select All, and Copy
display.afterImport: When the other device says it's done, tap Done. The QR will also clear on its own when the timer reaches zero.
display.fingerprintAriaLabel: Identity fingerprint to confirm on the other device

link.intro: On your new device, open Understoria and choose the option about already having your identity. Then type these 6 words there, in order:
link.warning: These words are the whole key for the next 15 minutes — whoever types them first gets your identity. Don't say them around others and never send them in a chat.
link.afterImport: When the new device says it's in, tap Done. The code dies on its own when the timer ends, and it only works once.
link.qrInstead: New device offline, or prefer nothing stored on the node? Show a QR instead
link.fallbackNotice: Couldn't reach your community node just now — using the QR method instead.

expired.title: Time's up.
expired.body: Five minutes passed. The QR is gone and so is the passphrase. If you still want to pair the other device, start over — a fresh passphrase will be generated.
expired.bodyLink: Fifteen minutes passed, so the words stopped working and the encrypted transfer was deleted from the node. If you still want to link the other device, start over — you'll get fresh words.

errors.title: Couldn't start pairing
errors.locked: Unlock your session before pairing — your identity key is sealed.

labelSource.title: Done. Want to label this for your records?
labelSource.body: A short note — like "Aunt's laptop" or "work phone" — helps you recognize this entry later in your Profile. The label stays on this device.

pairDevice.title: Pair this device
pairDevice.subtitle: Bring your identity over from the device that has it.
pairDevice.subtitleSamePhone: Bring your identity over from this phone's browser.

wait.badgeAriaLabel: Recognition emoji for this device's link request
wait.title: Approve from where you're signed in
wait.step1: On the device that has your identity, open Understoria.
wait.samePhoneStep1: Open Understoria in your browser — the one where you're signed in.
wait.waitingLine: Waiting for your approval… this ask expires in {{mmss}}.
wait.noNode: This device can't reach a community node, so tap-to-link isn't available. The QR method works without one.
wait.busyNode: A few devices are already asking to be linked from your network. Wait a minute for them to expire, then try again.
wait.nodeError: Your community's server isn't answering. Check your connection and try again.
wait.interfered: Something interfered with the transfer — what arrived wasn't from your approval, or was damaged. Nothing about your identity was exposed. Ask again — you'll get fresh emoji.
wait.vpnHint: Taking a while? If this phone uses a VPN or iCloud Private Relay, your two apps can look like they're on different networks and the ask never reaches your other screen. Pause it for a minute and tap "Ask again" — or use "Other ways to link" below.

linkIn.title: You're in as {{name}}.
linkIn.notMeConfirmBody: If that name isn't you, this device just received someone else's identity — likely someone on your network fooling around. Wiping clears this device completely (it held nothing of yours yet) so you can start over.
linkIn.wipeButton: Wipe this device and start over

otherWays.title: Other ways to link
otherWays.wordsTitle: Your other device is somewhere else?
otherWays.wordsBody: On that device, choose "Other ways to link" and get a 6-word code — then type the words here.
otherWays.qrTitle: No community server, or don't trust it?
otherWays.qrBody: Scan a QR straight off the other device's screen. Nothing touches any server.

link.directionsTitle: Get the 6 words from the device that has your identity.
link.step1: On that device, open Profile, then "Add another device," and Continue.
link.step2: It shows 6 words. Type them below, in order — they're valid for 15 minutes.
link.samePhoneStep1: In this phone's browser, open Understoria and go to Profile → "Add another device," then Continue.
link.samePhoneStep2: It shows 6 words. Flip back here and type them below — they stay valid for 15 minutes, so switching apps is fine.
link.notFound: Nothing waiting under these words. Check each word and the order — and check the other screen is still showing them. Codes expire after 15 minutes and each code works exactly once.
link.noNode: This device can't reach a community node, so word-linking isn't available here. Use the QR option below instead.
link.scanInstead: No community node, or the other device can't connect? Scan a QR instead

directions.title: Need a QR? Get one from your other device.
directions.step1: On the other device, open Profile, then "Add another device."
directions.step2: Follow the prompts — the QR appears with a 6-word code below it.
directions.step3: Hold the other device's screen up to this camera. It scans automatically. Or paste the QR contents into the box below if the camera can't see it.

capture.cameraInstructions: Point at the QR on the other device. It'll detect automatically.
capture.cameraUnavailableHelp: Your browser doesn't have a built-in QR reader, or you didn't allow camera access. You can paste the QR contents instead, if the other device offered them.
capture.videoAriaLabel: Live camera view, looking for a QR code
capture.invalidCode: That doesn't look like a pairing code. Copy it again from the other screen — make sure you get the whole thing.

samePhone.title: Bring your identity over from this phone's browser
samePhone.step1: Open Understoria in your browser — the one where you're signed in. Go to Profile, then "Add another device."
samePhone.step2: Write down the 6 words it shows. You'll type them here next — going back for another look can generate a new code, so paper beats memory.
samePhone.step3: Tap "Copy the code instead," then "Copy pairing code."
samePhone.step4: Come back here and tap the paste button below.
samePhone.clipboardFailed: Couldn't read the clipboard. Long-press the box below and paste there instead.
samePhone.switchToScan: Bringing your identity from a different device? Scan its QR instead
samePhone.switchToSamePhone: Is your identity in this same phone's browser? Follow the same-phone steps instead

passphrase.instructions: Type the {{count}} words from the other device, in order. They auto-complete as you type.

fingerprintConfirm.title: Does this match the other device?
fingerprintConfirm.body: The other device should be showing the same 8-character code under "Confirm on the other device." If it doesn't match, stop — something is wrong with the scan.
fingerprintConfirm.fingerprintAriaLabel: Identity fingerprint from the device you scanned
fingerprintConfirm.mismatchHint: "Start over" restarts the capture on this device. Ask the other device to start over too.

session.title: Lock this device with a passphrase
session.intro: Pick a passphrase to protect your identity on this device. You'll type it when the app asks you to unlock. Leave it blank to skip locking on this device — only do that if no one else has access.
session.skipHint: Leave both blank to skip — your key stays unwrapped on this device.

bootstrap.title: You're in.
bootstrap.intro: A few things this device starts without:

bullets.noDms: No DM history — messages are end-to-end encrypted to whichever device received them.
bullets.noDrafts: No drafts — the other device may have unsaved posts; those don't follow.
bullets.noPrefs: No theme or density preferences — set them in Profile → Settings if you want them to match.

bootstrap.outro: Achievements rebuild on their own as this device syncs with the community.

errors.lostEnvelope: The QR data is missing. Capture it again.
errors.malformed: That doesn't look like a pairing QR. Capture it again.
errors.wrongPassphrase: These words don't match. Check each one — order matters. If the other screen was reopened or refreshed, it generated a NEW code and new words: copy both again from the screen it's showing now.
errors.expired: This transfer is past its 5-minute window. Ask the other device to start over.
errors.versionMismatch: The other device is on a different version. Update both, then try again.
errors.publickeyMismatch: The QR's identity check didn't add up. Ask the other device to start over.

labelDestination.title: Want to label this device for your records?
labelDestination.body: A short note — like "my laptop" or "travel phone" — shows up on your Profile so you can see what's paired. The label stays on this device.

offline.banner: You're offline. Everything here still works from your device.
offline.guideLink: What you can still do in an outage
offline.pendingCount_one: {{count}} change will send when you reconnect.
offline.pendingCount_other: {{count}} changes will send when you reconnect.

toast.needPosted: Posted. Your need is live on the board.
toast.offerPosted: Posted. Your offer is live on the board.
toast.projectCreated: Project created. Add tasks and launch it when you're ready.
toast.exchangeConfirmedPending: Confirmed. Waiting for the other person to confirm too.
toast.exchangeConfirmedComplete: Confirmed. Credits have flowed between you.

common.copied: Copied. Share it over Signal, in person, or on paper.
common.copyFailed: Couldn't access the clipboard — select the link above and copy manually.

notFound.title: This page isn't here
notFound.message: The link may be old or mistyped. Nothing's wrong on your end.

drafts.bannerMessage: You started one of these {{when}}. Continue where you left off, or start fresh?

profileNudge.label: Profile completion
profileNudge.message: Members can find you for in-person help if you add a neighborhood or a skill or two. Optional, but it helps.
profileNudge.technical: Profile fields visible on your posts and member detail: skills (free-text comma-separated tokens, no taxonomy), zone (coarsened to neighborhood — never street address — so geographic context doesn't leak past what you explicitly shared), availability free-text notes, availability chips (coarse buckets — weekday days, weekday evenings, weekend days, weekend evenings, ask me anytime). The nudge dismisses when any of these is filled, or on explicit dismissal. Dismissal is per-device, not federated, and clears on hard purge.

firstActionNudge.label: Getting started
firstActionNudge.message: Two ways in: tap any post that catches your eye to see how exchange works, or share something you need or can offer. No pressure to post first — lurking is welcome too.
firstActionNudge.technical: The nudge appears on the Board until you take a first action — claiming a post, posting your own need or offer, or explicitly dismissing it. Once any of these happens, it stays dismissed per-device. A hard reset (Profile → Emergency → Hard purge) or a fresh install brings the nudge back; the state lives in local IndexedDB, not on the community node.

vouchNudge.label: Vouching is open to you
vouchNudge.title: You can vouch now
vouchNudge.body: Someone in the community vouched for you. You can do the same for people you trust — it's a way to expand who the community sees as trustworthy. You'll find the Vouch section on a member's page whenever your vouch would help them.

keepAccessNudge.label: Keeping your account safe
keepAccessNudge.title: Keep a spare copy of your account
keepAccessNudge.body: Your account lives on this device — that's what keeps it private. If you have a second device, pair it: your phone and a laptop can hold the same account, so losing one doesn't lock you out. It only takes a minute, and you do it with both devices in front of you.
keepAccessNudge.learnMore: There's no password to reset and no company that can restore your account — that's the trade for having no one able to lock you out or hand you over. A second paired device is the best way to keep access if a device is ever lost; a recovery kit (Settings → Recovery kit) is the paper backup for when you only have one device. (Saving your data export or setting a passphrase protects different things; neither one brings your account back on a new device.)

card.safariDesktopHint: In Safari: File menu → "Add to Dock" (macOS Sonoma or newer).

panel.title: Add Understoria to your phone
panel.intro: Add Understoria to your home screen and it opens like an app — full screen, offline-ready, no app store account.

oneTap.done: Added. You can open Understoria from your home screen now.

ios.step1: Tap the Share button at the bottom of the screen.
ios.step2: Scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen".
ios.step3: Tap "Add" in the top corner. Understoria appears with your other apps.

iosOther.title: Open this in Safari
iosOther.body: On an iPhone or iPad, only Safari can add an app to your home screen. Open this page in Safari, then add it from there.

desktop.step1: Look for the install icon at the right end of the address bar (Chrome or Edge).
desktop.step3: Understoria opens in its own window, with your other apps.

inAppBrowser.title: Open this in your browser first
inAppBrowser.body: You're viewing Understoria inside another app, which can't add it to your home screen. Open this page in Safari or Chrome, then add it from there.
inAppBrowser.hint: Look for "Open in browser" in this app's menu (often the ⋯ or share icon).

step.title: Add Understoria to your phone
step.intro: Optional, but handy: add Understoria to your home screen so it opens like an app and works offline. You can always do this later from Profile → Learn.

board.label: Board orientation
board.message: Three tabs at the top: Needs (what people are asking for), Offers (what people can share), and Projects (group goals). Tap a post to see more or to claim it. The Board hides posts someone's already claimed, so you see what still needs help.

balance.label: Understanding your balance
balance.message: Your balance is hours of help. Everyone starts with 5 so you can ask for help right away, even before you've given any. One hour of any kind of help equals one hour. Give and receive at your own pace.

invite.label: Growing the community
invite.message: Communities grow through trust. When you invite someone, you're vouching for them — your name backs their join. New members need two vouches before they appear as 'trusted' on the Board. Share invites with people you know.

calendar.title: Community calendar
calendar.tagline: What the community is doing together — project deadlines, posts about to expire, and the rhythm of exchanges that already happened.

density.tooltipBody: No member names appear here. The count is the community's metabolism, not anyone's score.

empty.title: The community's calendar is quiet this week.
empty.body: When someone creates a project with a deadline or posts a need that expires, it'll show up here. Activity from completed exchanges shows up too — the calendar reflects what the community is doing together, not a score to chase. Know of something the community should gather for? The + button below starts an event.

templates.title: Pick a starting point
templates.intro: These are starter shapes for common get-togethers. Pick one to fill in the form below — you can edit anything before you sign. Or start from scratch.
templates.selected: Starting from the {{name}} template — edit anything below before you sign.

search.ariaLabel: Search event templates by name or description

filters.empty: No templates match. Try a different search, or start from scratch below.

new.workDayBannerBody: This event will appear on the project page on this node. On peer nodes it appears as a regular community event — the project connection never federates. The title below is yours to edit before you sign.
new.workDayDescriptionPrefill: A hands-on work session for {{project}}. Come for any part of it.
new.title: Create event
new.subtitle: An event is a signed, federated record. Names what you're committing to before you sign.
new.descriptionPlaceholder: What's happening, what to bring, who it's for.
new.locationHint: Free text. Don't put a literal address that you wouldn't publish — every peer node will see this.
new.signingMeansOrganizer: Your public key is published as the organizer. Anyone with federation access can see you organized this.
new.signingMeansPublic: The location and start time you enter are visible on every peer node. Choose a description that matches what you can publish.
new.signingMeansPermanent: Events are append-only. You can cancel; you cannot delete or edit. A cancellation is itself a signed, permanent record.
new.signingMeansRsvp: RSVPs from members on this node are visible to you and to other RSVPs on this node only. Members on other nodes never see the roster.
new.signingNotEditable: Editable — to change the time or location, cancel and re-create.
new.signingNotPrivate: Privately scoped — there is no "members only" event in phase 1. If it shouldn't be on a public wire, coordinate over DM or a NEED/OFFER post instead.
new.errorGeneric: Couldn't create the event. Please try again.
new.errorTitleRequired: Give your event a title so members know what they'd be coming to.
new.errorLocationRequired: Add a location — even a casual one like "the library bench".
new.errorStartRequired: Pick a start date and time.
new.errorStartInPast: Event start time is in the past. Pick a time later than now.
new.errorEndInvalid: End time isn't valid. Pick a date and time, or remove the end-time toggle.
new.errorEndBeforeStart: End time has to be after the start time.
new.errorEndBeforeStartMidnightHint: Runs past midnight? Tick “Ends on a different day.”
new.errorCapacityInvalid: Capacity must be a positive whole number, or leave it empty for uncapped.
new.errorLocked: Unlock this device with your passphrase before signing the event.

detail.notFound: We couldn't find that event on this node.
detail.addToCalendarHint: Downloads a file for your device's calendar. Reminders are up to you and your calendar app.
detail.cancelHint: Cancellation is the only post-create change in phase 1. The cancellation itself is a signed, permanent record; members who RSVP'd will see the reason on their attention rail.
detail.cancelDialogBody: This signs a cancellation record. It federates to peer nodes and cannot be undone. RSVP'd members will see this on their attention rail.
detail.cancelReasonPlaceholder: Why is this changing? A sentence is plenty.
detail.cancelLocked: Unlock this device with your passphrase before signing the cancellation.
detail.rosterHiddenHint: RSVP "Going" or "Maybe" to see who else is coming. Everyone else sees the count only.

rsvp.heading: RSVP to this event
rsvp.signingBodyGoing: If you choose Going or Maybe, the organizer and other RSVP'd members will see your name on this event.
rsvp.signingBodyNotGoing: If you choose Not going, your choice is private — only you can see it.
rsvp.signingBodyLocalOnly: Your RSVP stays on this node. It is NOT federated to peer nodes.

shifts.emptyOrganizerHint: Break the day into shifts so members can see where hands are needed.
shifts.fullHint: This shift is full. If you can still help, check with the organizer.
shifts.rosterHiddenHint: Names are visible to the organizer and to members who RSVP'd going or maybe.
shifts.addToCalendar: Add this shift to my calendar (.ics)
shifts.consentRsvp: This also RSVPs you “Going” to the event.
shifts.consentRoster: The organizer and members who RSVP'd going or maybe will see your name on this shift's roster. Everyone else here sees spot counts only — other communities see neither.
shifts.consentRemove: You can remove your signup any time, in one tap. Your name comes off immediately. Nobody is notified — plans change.
shifts.recorded: You're signed up. Saved on this device.
shifts.capacityFloorHint_one: {{count}} person signed up — you can't set fewer spots than that.

company.heading: Company makes starting easier
company.why: Working alongside someone — even on completely different things — helps many people start and stick with a task. Some call it body doubling.
company.how: Opens a ready-to-edit board post. You choose every word, and nothing is shared until you post it.

task.releaseReassurance: Releasing helps the organizer find another helper — no judgment.
task.notClaimablePlanning: This project is still planning — the organizer hasn't launched it yet.
task.notClaimablePaused: This project is paused — no new claims right now.
task.notClaimableOther: This project isn't accepting claims right now.
task.claimableAfterLaunch: Members will be able to claim this once you launch the project.

addFreshCopy.hint: Adds an open copy of this task at the bottom of the list — handy for work that recurs.
addFreshCopy.toast: Added a fresh “{{title}}” at the bottom of the task list.

actualHours.hint: Record the real time — the credit should match the help given. No judgment either way.

claimerNarrative.intro: You've marked this done. An organizer confirms to complete the exchange — your {{hours}} hours of credit move then.
claimerNarrative.autoConfirm_one: If no one gets to it, the node confirms automatically within about {{count}} day.
claimerNarrative.autoConfirm_other: If no one gets to it, the node confirms automatically within about {{count}} days.

confirmDialog.title: Confirm this task?

But this doesn't have to mean starting over: another linked device still holds your identity, and if you made a recovery kit or chose guardians (Settings → Recovery kit / Guardians), you can wipe this app (Profile → Emergency → Hard purge) and restore from them on the fresh start. recoveryKit.title: Recovery kit
recoveryKit.intro: A small file (or printed page) that can bring your account back on a brand-new device if this one is ever lost and you have no paired spare. It holds your secret key, locked under a passphrase you choose right now.
recoveryKit.passphraseIntro: Choose a recovery passphrase. It is separate from any passphrase you use day to day — and there is nobody who can reset it: a kit without its passphrase is just paper. Whoever holds BOTH the kit and this passphrase can act as you.
recoveryKit.created: Your kit is ready. Save the file somewhere that isn't this device, or print it — a drawer at home beats a folder on this phone.
recoveryKit.storageHint: Store it like a spare house key: out of sight, away from this device, and where you'll find it in a bad week. Make a new kit if you change communities.
recoveryKit.errorLocked: This device is locked — unlock it first so the kit can include your key.
recoveryKit.errorNoIdentity: No identity on this device to back up.
recoveryKit.printQrAlt: QR code holding this recovery kit — scan it from the Understoria welcome screen to restore
recoveryKit.printIntro: This page can restore {{name}}'s Understoria account on a new device: open the app, choose "I have a recovery kit", scan this code with any QR scanner and paste the text, then enter the recovery passphrase. Keep this page somewhere safe — with the passphrase, it IS the account.
recoveryKit.printFooter: No company holds a copy of this. That is the point — and it means this page and its passphrase are the only way back without another paired device.

recover.title: Restore from a recovery kit
recover.intro: If you made a recovery kit on your old device, it brings your account — balance, vouches, roles, membership — onto this one. Your community's shared history syncs back from its server afterwards.
recover.uploadBody: The understoria-recovery-kit-….json file you downloaded when you made the kit.
recover.pasteLink: Printed kit? Scan the QR with any scanner app and paste the text here →
recover.kitFound: Found a kit for {{name}}. Enter its recovery passphrase to restore.
recover.errorNotAKit: That doesn't look like an Understoria recovery kit.
recover.errorVersion: This kit was made by a newer version of the app — update and try again.
recover.errorPassphrase: That passphrase doesn't open this kit.
recover.errorLocked: This device is locked — unlock it before restoring.
recover.errorCorrupted: This kit is damaged — the key inside doesn't match its owner. Try the downloaded file instead of a retyped copy.
recover.guardiansBody: If you chose guardians in your community, enough of them together can bring your account back — no kit or passphrase needed.
recover.guardiansIntro: Show this code to each of your guardians in person (or read it to them over a call you both trust). Each guardian's app answers with a release code — capture each one here. Once enough pieces are together, your account comes back.
recover.requestQrAlt: Recovery request code — show this to each guardian

guardians.title: Guardians
guardians.intro: Split your key among trusted members so that if you lose every device AND your recovery kit, enough of them together can bring you back. No single guardian — and no server — ever holds enough to act as you.
guardians.current: Any {{threshold}} of your {{total}} guardians can recover your account: {{names}}.
guardians.reshardHonesty: Choosing new guardians doesn't cancel the old set — your key itself doesn't change, so enough OLD shards together could still recover it. If a former guardian worries you, make a fresh account instead.
guardians.needMembers: You need at least {{min}} other members in your community to set this up.
guardians.pickWarning: Pick people who know your face and voice — their only job in a recovery is to make sure it's really you asking. Any group of them at the threshold size can reconstruct your key, so choose people who wouldn't collude.
guardians.thresholdLabel: How many must agree to recover you?
guardians.showToHint: Hand-off is device-to-device: they scan it from your screen (Settings → Guardians → Accept a shard) or you paste it to them over a channel you both trust. Nothing goes through a server.
guardians.noDuties: Nobody has handed you a shard yet. When someone chooses you as a guardian, accept their code here.
guardians.accepted: You now hold piece {{index}} of {{total}} for {{name}}. Any {{threshold}} guardians together can recover them — your piece alone reveals nothing.
guardians.releaseFriction: Stop. Are you CERTAIN this is really {{name}}? Someone pretending to be them — a message from a new number, a voice on a rushed call — is exactly how this gets stolen. Meet them, see their face, or hear their voice on a call YOU placed before going on.
guardians.releaseCaptureHint: Capture the recovery request code from their new device.
guardians.releaseReady: Show this release code to them — it only works on the device that made the request you just scanned.
guardians.lockedHint: This device is locked — unlock it to use guardian features.

error.locked: This device is locked — unlock it first.
error.bad_params: That combination doesn't work — you can't guard yourself, each guardian counts once, and the threshold must be at least 2 and no more than the number of guardians.
error.not_a_shard: That code isn't a guardian shard.
error.not_addressed_to_me: That shard is addressed to a different member — each guardian must scan their own code.
error.undecryptable: That code can't be opened here — it was sealed for someone else, or it doesn't match this request.
error.no_duty: You don't hold a shard for that member.
error.bad_request: That doesn't look like a recovery request code.
error.not_a_release: That code isn't a shard release.
error.different_owner: That release is for a different member's account.
error.different_set: That release comes from an older (or newer) set of guardians and can't be combined with the pieces you already have. All releases must come from the same set — ask the guardians which is their latest.
error.corrupted: The pieces don't reconstruct this member's key — one may be damaged or from a different set. Re-capture them and try again.
error.device_locked: This device is locked — unlock it before restoring.

removals.title: Removals and reinstatements
removals.intro: The heaviest decisions a community can make, recorded in the open: who was removed or welcomed back, when, why, and exactly who signed. No single person — not even the server's operator — can create one of these; each needs a quorum of members co-signing.
removals.interstitial: Removing {{name}} is the heaviest thing this community can do, and it can't be done alone — it takes several members each signing their name to one public record. Before going further: a personal block already stops their content from reaching you, a dispute can contest a specific exchange, and a conversation has mended more communities than any tool here. If it still has to be this, write a reason the whole community will read.
removals.reinstateIntro: Reinstating {{name}} reopens the door — their reading and writing come back, though invites that died with the removal stay dead. It takes the same number of signatures as a removal. You can add a note the community will read.
removals.reasonPlaceholder: Written for the community, not a case file.
removals.linkLabel: Link the proposal the community deliberated (optional)
removals.linkedProposalMissing: This draft references a community proposal that isn't on this device yet.
removals.draftHint: Show this to each member who agrees, in person. Their device signs and answers with a code — capture each one below. Nothing is submitted until enough have signed; you can stop at any time and nothing happened.
removals.draftQrAlt: Removal draft code — show to each co-signer
removals.submitted: Submitted. Once the community's server accepts it, the record appears below and takes effect on every member's device on the next sync.
removals.cosignCaptureHint: Capture the draft code from the proposing member's screen.
removals.cosignFriction: You are being asked to co-sign REMOVING {{name}} from the community. Your name goes on the record, permanently and publicly. Sign only if you've weighed the lighter tools and believe the community needs this.
removals.cosignReinstateFriction: You are being asked to co-sign REINSTATING {{name}}. Your name goes on the record, permanently and publicly.
removals.cosignShowBack: Show this to the proposing member — their device collects it toward the quorum.
removals.selfBanner: This community has removed you by quorum decision. Everything on this device is still yours and keeps working here, but syncing with the community's server will fail until a quorum of members signs a reinstatement. The decision, its reason, and its signers are on the Decisions page.

reseed.title: Restore this community onto a node
reseed.intro: If every community server is ever lost, this device still holds the shared history — every post, exchange, event, project, and membership record, cryptographically signed. Point it at a fresh node and upload it all back. Several members can do this at once; copies simply combine.
reseed.systemKeyNote: For the new node's operator — the lost node's auto-confirm key (TRUSTED_SYSTEM_KEYS):
reseed.confirmBody: Everything this device holds of the community's shared records will be uploaded to {{url}} — the same data any node of this community holds. Only continue if that address is your community's replacement server. This can take a few minutes and resumes if interrupted.
reseed.doneInterrupted: Restore paused — run it again to continue from where it stopped.
reseed.skippedHint: Skipped records are ones this node declined — usually auto-confirmed exchanges (the operator can enable them with TRUSTED_SYSTEM_KEYS) or receipts outside an open re-seed window (RESEED_GRACE_UNTIL). See docs/community-reseed.md.
reseed.windowedNote: This device freed up space and keeps the last {{years}} year(s) plus everything active and yours — it will restore what it holds, and other members' devices fill in the rest.

mirrorSuggest.label: Add a backup server for this community
mirrorSuggest.title: Add a backup server for this community?
mirrorSuggest.body: Your community's server announced a second server it runs alongside it: {{origin}}. If you add it, your app switches to it on its own whenever the usual server can't be reached, and what you share syncs there too. Whoever runs the backup can see the same community data as your usual server's operator. Nothing is used until you confirm.

growRootSuggest.label: Our community's node is running low on room
growRootSuggest.title: Our community's node is running low on room
growRootSuggest.body: Growing another root would spread the load. If you run this community's server, it may just need more storage or a chance to shed older records; either way, a second server relieves the pressure and helps everyone. Nothing happens until you decide.

growRoot.title: Grow another root
growRoot.intro: Understoria communities get stronger the way trees do — by growing more roots. Pick the way you can help.

gate.title: This guide opens with trust
gate.body: Running a server for the community comes with real responsibilities — whoever runs one becomes an operator, with powers and limits the whole community can read about. So this guided path opens for trusted members: people vouched for by at least {{count}} others, the same trust bar the app uses everywhere else.
gate.progress: You have {{have}} of {{need}} vouches so far. That's not a grade — it simply grows as you exchange help with neighbors.
gate.seedVaultLead: Something you can do right now: pledging a complete copy of the community's archive is open to everyone, no server required.
gate.seedVaultLink: Keep the complete archive on this device
gate.guideLink: Reading the full guide is open to everyone too

run.title: I can run a computer that stays on
run.body: An old laptop in a closet is genuinely enough. This guide generates the settings for you and checks the result with you.

ask.title: I want to ask someone
ask.body: Compose a short note for the friend with the spare computer — you bring the invitation, the app brings the steps.

help.title: I can't run a server, but I can help
help.body: There's a zero-ops way to strengthen your community's roots from the device already in your hand.

ask.title: Ask someone to grow a root
ask.body: Here's a note ready to send. Your usual channel is the right one — there's nothing secret in it.
ask.message: Hi! Our community coordinates on {{origin}} — one hour of help equals one hour of help. Right now everything runs on a single server, and we're looking for one more person to run a second one, so no single machine (and no single person) can be a pressure point. An old laptop that stays on is genuinely enough, and the app walks you through every step: {{guide}}. Would you be up for it? It's about an afternoon, and I'll help.

helper.title: Help without running a server
helper.body: Every member's device already carries a signed copy of the community, but phones trim old records when space runs low. Pledging a seed vault means one of your devices keeps the complete archive — no port forwarding, no operator powers, just storage and solidarity. If every server were ever lost, seed vaults are the devices the community restores from.
helper.link: Open the data settings to pledge

needs.title: What you need
needs.intro: Three things, none of them exotic:
needs.computer: A computer that can stay on — an old laptop (lid shut, in a closet), a mini-PC, or a Raspberry-Pi-class board.
needs.internet: A home internet connection, and the ability to forward a port on your router or use a tunneling service.
needs.afternoon: An afternoon, plus a conversation with whoever runs your community's current server.
needs.ack: I've read what running a node means: an operator holds the powers and limits named in docs/operator-powers.md, and holding them is visible to my community.
needs.ackLink: Read about it on the Help page

settings.title: Your community's settings
settings.loading: Asking your community's server for its details…
settings.noNode: This device isn't connected to a community server yet, so the guide can't read your community's details. Connect one in Settings first.
settings.intro: This is the settings file for the new server, generated for your community. Copy it whole — the lines that still need a human are called out below.
settings.readAuth: Our community requires signed reads (READ_AUTH is on)
settings.readAuthHint: Ask your current operator if you're not sure. The read gate must match on every server — a mirror with it off would serve the whole replicated dataset to anyone with its address.
settings.placeholdersLead: Before the server starts, three lines need a human:
settings.placeholderDbKey: DATABASE_KEY — run the command in the comment on the new computer and paste the result.
settings.placeholderFounders: NODE_FOUNDER_KEYS — copy it from your current operator, character for character.
settings.placeholderQuorum: REMOVAL_QUORUM — set it to the same number the current server uses.

handover.title: Hand this to your current operator
handover.body: The current server needs a few lines to pair with the new one and announce it to members' apps. Show the QR code in person, or paste the text.
handover.urlHint: Ask the new operator where the server will live — a domain or tunnel address like https://node2.example.
handover.label: Settings for the current server
handover.qrAlt: QR code carrying the settings block for the current operator
handover.tokenNote: The same token appears in both blocks on purpose — it's how the two servers will recognize each other. It travels only by this copy button or QR code, never over the network.

verify.title: Check your new root
verify.intro: Once the new server is running and both settings files are in place, put its address here and let's look at it together.

check.distinctNodeId: It has a name of its own
check.announced: Your current server announces it to members

verify.notYet: Not all green yet — completely normal on a first try. Adjust what the row names, restart the server, and run the checks again; retrying breaks nothing.
verify.allGreen: Every member's app will now offer the new root on a consent card — including yours. Your community just became harder to take down.

trust.trustedTooltip: This member has at least two valid vouches.
trust.pendingTooltip: Fewer than two vouches so far. They can still post needs and offers.

first_exchange.label: First Exchange
first_exchange.description: You completed your first exchange — giving or receiving.

connector_5.label: Connector
connector_5.description: You've helped 5 different people in our community.

regular_4weeks.label: Regular
regular_4weeks.description: You've been active for 4 consecutive weeks — at least one exchange each week.

bridge_builder.label: Bridge Builder
bridge_builder.description: You fulfilled a need in a category no one had filled before.

seed_planter.label: Seed Planter
seed_planter.description: 3 people you invited have each completed an exchange.

listener.label: Listener
listener.description: You've completed 3 exchanges supporting someone emotionally.

weaver.label: Weaver
weaver.description: Your exchanges have connected members across 3 or more areas of our community.

groundbreaker.label: Groundbreaker
groundbreaker.description: You launched a community project that drew at least one contributor.

crew_member.label: Crew Member
crew_member.description: You completed 3 or more confirmed tasks across community projects.

momentum_maker.label: Momentum Maker
momentum_maker.description: A project you organized reached the halfway mark of its target hours.

keystone.label: Keystone
keystone.description: A project you organized was completed.

attention.title: Needs your attention
attention.intro: A few things are waiting on you. Tap to handle them when you have a moment.
attention.tapToConfirm: Tap to confirm and release the credits.
attention.tapToConfirmTask: Tap to confirm and credit the helper.

taskCheckIn.line: Still on “{{task}}” for {{project}}? You claimed it {{when}}.
taskCheckIn.hint: Capacity changes — release it if you need to. No record kept.

postClaimed.hint: A helper is on the way. Tap to see the post.

vouchReceived.hint: Each vouch brings you closer to trusted status.

growRoot.line: Our community's node is running low on room.
growRoot.hint: Another root would ease the pressure — tap to help.

projectPaused.line: {{project}} has been paused for a while.
projectPaused.hint: Resume it or mark it complete when ready.

coorgInvitation.hint: This commits your balance and identity to confirming work on the project. Take a look before you accept.
coorgInvitation.lockedToRespond: Unlock this device with your passphrase before responding.

accept.title: Accept co-organizer role for "{{project}}"
accept.meansConfirm: Confirming task completions — each confirmation moves hours out of your own balance.
accept.meansSign: Signing as a project organizer — records you sign in this role trace back to your identity in the project's federated history.
accept.meansVisible: Visible to the community — the project page lists you as a co-organizer on every peer's view.
accept.notObligation: No standing obligation — you can step back from the role yourself at any time.
accept.notDeputy: No authority over the primary — the primary organizer keeps handoff and removal authority. Co-organizers are peers, not deputies.

decline.confirmBody: No role is assigned. The organizer sees that you declined the next time they look at the project.

board.title: Community board
board.tagline: Post what you need. Offer what you can.

oneSmallThing.heading: One small thing
oneSmallThing.why: For low-energy days: skip the choosing. One bounded thing you could pick up — drawn at random from what's open right now, never ranked, never based on your history.
oneSmallThing.empty: Nothing open fits right now — the tabs above have the full picture.

empty.needs: No needs match these filters yet. If you need help, post something — asking is never gated in a timebank.
empty.offers: No offers match these filters yet. What could you share with your community this week?

dashboard.title: Community dashboard
dashboard.tagline: The unit of progress is us, not me.

resume.title: Pick up where you left off
resume.hint: The last task you touched — your own navigation memory, kept on this device only.

handsWelcome.title: Where hands are welcome
handsWelcome.intro: A few open doors, for whenever you have capacity.

stats.seeOpenNeedsAria: See open needs on the community board

categoryBreakdown.title: Where help is flowing
categoryBreakdown.empty: No exchanges yet. Once the first one happens, you'll see the mix of care, labor, and support flowing through the community.

resilience.title: Community resilience
resilience.bodySeedling: Right now this community lives on this device alone. Connecting to a community server — or starting one — lets everyone's copies stay in step.
resilience.bodyOne: One server carries this community — and {{count}} member carries it on their own device too. Nothing here can be taken away by taking a machine.
resilience.bodyOther: One server carries this community — and every one of its {{count}} members carries it on their own device too. Nothing here can be taken away by taking a machine.
resilience.bodyMultiOne: {{nodes}} servers carry this community — and {{count}} member carries it on their own device too. Nothing here can be taken away by taking a machine.
resilience.bodyMultiOther: {{nodes}} servers carry this community — and every one of its {{count}} members carries it on their own device too. Nothing here can be taken away by taking a machine.
resilience.bodyFailover: If one server disappears, your apps switch to the others on their own. Nothing is lost.
resilience.windowedNote: This device keeps the last {{years}} year(s) plus everything active and yours — the servers keep the full history.
resilience.seedVaultsOne: {{count}} member keeps the complete archive on a device of their own — a seed vault.
resilience.seedVaultsOther: {{count}} members keep the complete archive on devices of their own — seed vaults.
resilience.nodeQuiet: No server has answered lately — your copy is safe, and syncing resumes when one is back.
resilience.capacityRed: Our community's node is running low on room — growing another root would ease the pressure.

breadth.title: Breadth, not depth
breadth.intro: A glimpse of how widely help spreads — each bar is one member's reach across the community. Generosity spread widely, not piled deep.
breadth.empty: No exchanges yet. The first one will start the pattern.
breadth.moreNote: {{count}} more {{noun}} have reached at least one person — every name in the community would belong here if we listed them all.

reciprocity.title: Reciprocity
reciprocity.intro: Connections that flow both ways — A has helped B and B has helped A.
reciprocity.footnote: Not a score. Just a window into the shape of help across the community.

member.notFound: Member not found on this device.
member.vouchIntro: Vouching is a signed statement that this person is part of our community. Two distinct vouches (including invite redemption) mark a member as trusted.
member.vouchedJustNow: Vouched. Your signed statement is in the local ledger and (if a community node is configured) on its way there.
member.cannotVouchUntilTrusted: Vouching is enabled once you're trusted yourself. Your inviter's vouch is one; another community member can vouch for you to make it two.
member.alreadyFullyTrusted: This member is already trusted. Another vouch wouldn't change anything.
member.alreadyVouchedByYou: You've already vouched for this member.
member.recordDirectHint: Helped each other outside any post or project? Record the hours together — you both sign, phone to phone.

settings.title: Settings
settings.intro: How Understoria behaves on this device. Changes here only affect this device — they don't sync across the community.

pilotJournal.title: Pilot journal
pilotJournal.intro: Notes for the pilot, kept on this device. Jot down anything that confused you, broke, or worked well — whatever would help the people running this pilot make it better.
pilotJournal.privacyNote: Nothing here is sent anywhere. It stays on this device until you delete it, or hand it over yourself: tap “Share my journal” to save a plain-text copy you can give the organizer or read aloud at a check-in.
pilotJournal.placeholder: e.g. “Took me a while to find where to confirm a task”
pilotJournal.empty: No notes yet. Add one whenever something's worth remembering.
pilotJournal.helpDoorway: Keeping a note of what's confusing or what works helps the pilot.

invitesPage.title: Your invites
invitesPage.intro: Invites you've issued. Open ones come first; you can copy, re-show the QR, or revoke any of them.

empty.title: No invites issued yet
empty.message: Generate your first invite from your Profile.

fullKey.title: Your full public key
fullKey.hint: This key is public — it's safe to share, print, or paste anywhere. It's how servers and other members recognize you. If you operate your community's server, this exact value is what goes in NODE_FOUNDER_KEYS in its .env file.

addDevice.title: Add another device
addDevice.subtitle: Use the same identity on a second device — phone, laptop, or both.

pairingLog.title: Paired devices
pairingLog.intro: Pair flows you've completed on this device — for your memory. This list lives only on this device.
pairingLog.rotateHint: To rotate your identity entirely (e.g. if a device is lost), see Emergency below.
pairingLog.destinationTitle: Times this device was authorized from elsewhere

balance.label: Your balance
balance.footerNote: Balances can go negative — asking is never gated.
balance.surplus: You've given a lot lately. Communities stay healthy when helpers ask too — what would help you?
balance.neutral: Your balance is right at your starting seed. That's a fine place to be.
balance.receiving: You've been receiving — that's what seed credits are for. Ask for what you need.
balance.pendingExplainerAuto_one: Credit moves when both sides confirm an exchange. If a partner doesn't get to it, the community node confirms for them within about {{count}} day — nothing is stuck.
balance.pendingExplainerAuto_other: Credit moves when both sides confirm an exchange. If a partner doesn't get to it, the community node confirms for them within about {{count}} days — nothing is stuck.
balance.pendingExplainerManual: Credit moves when both sides confirm an exchange. Nothing is stuck — it lands the moment the second confirmation happens.
balance.projectOutflowLine: Includes {{hours}} you moved to helpers on behalf of {{project}}.
balance.projectOutflowLineMore_one: Includes {{hours}} you moved to helpers on behalf of {{project}} and {{count}} other project.
balance.projectOutflowLineMore_other: Includes {{hours}} you moved to helpers on behalf of {{project}} and {{count}} other projects.

about.title: About you
about.avatarNote: This little plant is your identity, drawn from your public key. Everyone with the same key sees the same plant. It can't be changed — that's how others recognize you across the app.
about.name: Display name (pseudonym is fine)
about.availabilitySubhead: Optional. Pick what fits, or leave empty — 'Ask me anytime' is fine too.

rolesEarned.title: Your community roles
rolesEarned.empty: No named roles yet. They show up as you participate — not as trophies, but as ways of naming the shapes your contributions take.

history.title: Your exchange history
history.empty: Nothing here yet. When you give or receive help, each exchange shows up with a signed record you can verify.
history.flagShort: Very short exchange — surfaced for community review.
history.flagReciprocal: Repeated reciprocal exchange with the same person — surfaced for community review.
history.directFlagTitle: Flag this exchange for community review
history.directFlagBody: This exchange was recorded directly — phone to phone, with no post behind it. Flagging surfaces it on the Disputes page where the whole community can see it and respond. There are no admins; this starts a conversation, not a punishment.

memberSwitcher.title: Switch member (local dev)
memberSwitcher.note: In a real deployment each device holds one identity. This switcher exists so you can walk through the full exchange flow yourself while testing.

data.title: Data & privacy
data.intro: Everything you see is stored locally on this device. No server. No analytics. No account to sign up for.
data.storageUnprotected: the browser may clear it under disk pressure — installing the app usually protects it
data.storageUnknown: cleanup protection unknown on this browser

window.full: This device carries the community's full history.
window.windowed: This device keeps the last {{years}} year(s) plus everything active and everything yours. The full history stays on your community's servers.
window.freeUp: Free up space on this device…
window.chooseHorizon: How much history should this device keep?
window.preview: This removes {{total}} old records from this device only: {{posts}} settled posts, {{events}} past events, {{projects}} closed projects, and their details.
window.previewNote: Nothing you wrote, nothing still open, and none of the community's ledger, roster, or membership records are touched. Every removed record stays on your community's servers and on other members' devices — you can bring everything back later.
window.done: Done — {{total}} records removed from this device.
window.undoDone: This device will re-download the full history in the background. Activity logs of long-closed projects don't come back — they only ever lived on devices.

vault.body: Got room to spare — an old laptop, a tablet in a drawer? Pledge to keep the complete archive on this device. It's the zero-ops way to make the community harder to erase: no server, no port forwarding, just storage and solidarity. Your pledge is public so the community can see how many full copies exist; you can stop any time.
vault.enable: Keep the complete archive on this device
vault.active: This device is a seed vault — it keeps the community's complete archive.
vault.enabled: Pledged. This device now keeps everything (any freed-up space is being re-downloaded).
vault.disabled: Pledge withdrawn. Nothing was deleted — this device just no longer promises to keep everything.

error.locked: This device is locked — unlock it first.

learn.title: Learn
learn.rowDescription: Guides, common questions, and design principles — everything works offline.
learn.intro: The welcome flow, common questions, a condensed member guide, and prompts for study groups. Everything here works offline.
learn.opsecIntro: Mutual aid work carries real risks. This short guide covers the basics of keeping yourself and your community safe. Read it once; come back when something changes.
learn.principlesIntro: Every design choice in Understoria has a reason. These principles explain why the app works the way it does, with examples of what happened when other projects tried it differently.

communitySettings.title: Community settings
communitySettings.rowDescription: Open to review or tune the community's thresholds.
communitySettings.intro: Thresholds the community can tune to fit its own context. Defaults work for most groups.
communitySettings.bootstrapNote: Direct edits here take effect immediately for everyone on this device; settings changes can also go through a community proposal and vote instead.
communitySettings.proposeLink: Or propose a change for the community to discuss →

dailyLimit.label: Daily helper limit
dailyLimit.hint: The most exchanges any single helper can complete in 24 hours. A hard stop, not a guideline.

shortExchange.label: Short-exchange flag threshold (hours)
shortExchange.hint: Exchanges shorter than this get an advisory flag for community review. Not blocked, not punished — just surfaced.

reciprocal.label: Reciprocal-pattern threshold
reciprocal.hint: When the same two members exchange this many times in 30 days, the latest exchange gets an advisory flag.

taskCheckInDays.label: Task check-in (days)
taskCheckInDays.hint: After this many days, the claimer of a project task gets a private 'still on it?' prompt in their attention list. They can release without anyone seeing.

taskNeedsHelpDays.label: Task 'could use more hands' (days)
taskNeedsHelpDays.hint: After this many days, a claimed task is publicly marked as needing more hands. Framed at the task, not the person. Must be at least the check-in threshold.

taskCheckInGraceDays.label: Task check-in grace (days)
taskCheckInGraceDays.hint: After the claimer acknowledges the private nudge, suppress the public 'could use more hands' chip for this many days. Lets a responsive claimer keep the task out of community signalling. Keep small (1-3) so prolonged silence still surfaces.

deliberationDays.label: Proposal deliberation period (days)
deliberationDays.hint: Minimum days a proposal must stay open before it can auto-pass on consensus. Prevents rush votes.

minAffirms.label: Minimum affirm votes
minAffirms.hint: Distinct affirm votes required for a proposal to auto-pass. Even with deliberation done, a proposal that only one person has affirmed shouldn't pass silently.

inviteOnly.label: Invite-only onboarding
inviteOnly.helpText: When enabled, visitors to /welcome see an invite-only landing. The first member to onboard on a fresh node is the exception (bootstrap).

language.title: Language
language.intro: Choose how Understoria speaks to you on this device. More languages and translations of the rest of the app are coming as the i18n migration proceeds.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.intro: Light or dark. Match system follows your device — useful if you've set your OS to switch by time of day.
appearance.textSizeIntro: Multiplies with your device's text size — if you've made it larger system-wide, the larger options here stack on top.
appearance.autoSublabel: On this screen, Auto shows {{resolved}}. Resize the window to see it switch.
appearance.densityIntro: Compact trims card padding so denser screens fit more above the fold. Touch targets stay the same size.

node.title: Community node
node.intro: Optionally mirror your finalized exchanges to a community node so they show up in the community-wide ledger. The node verifies every signature; it never stores your private key.
node.enableLabel: Mirror finalized exchanges to this node
node.inviteLink: Have an invite link? Open it here.

consent.title: Send your exchange records to this server?
consent.body: Turning on mirroring sends every exchange you take part in to this server — including the other person's public key, the signature, category, hours, and time. A server you don't control can use this to map who-helps-whom across your community. Only enable this for a node your community runs and trusts.

invites.title: Invites you've issued
invites.intro: A new member needs two vouches to become trusted. Your invite counts as the first — someone else will need to vouch for them after they join.
invites.empty: You haven't invited anyone yet. When you do, the invite link will show up here so you can re-share it or revoke it.
invites.shareTitle: Share this link with one person
invites.shareNote: The link is single-use and expires in 14 days. If it leaks before redemption, revoke it below.
invites.usedAfterRevoke: Used {{when}} by {{name}}, after you revoked it. It still admits no one — but it may be worth a conversation.
invites.revealedHint: Anyone in camera view can read this. Hide it again when you're done.

shareSheet.title: Share this invite
shareSheet.intro: Show the QR code in person, or send the link with one person you trust. Anyone with this link can join — share it carefully.
shareSheet.qrAriaLabel: QR code containing the invite link
shareSheet.qrFallback: Couldn't draw the QR code. Use the link below instead.
shareSheet.shareText: I'm inviting you to join my community on Understoria.
shareSheet.statusFailed: Couldn't share or copy. Select the link and copy it manually.

cameraGate.title: Look around before you show this
cameraGate.body: Security cameras and webcams can read QR codes from across a room. Once it's on screen, anyone in camera view can save it and use it to join your community themselves.
cameraGate.followup: This prompt comes up every time — your surroundings can change between one share and the next.
cameraGate.shareWithoutShowing: Send the link without showing it
cameraGate.notAvailable: Your browser can't share or copy without showing the link. Tap "Show the invite" and copy it by hand instead.
cameraGate.statusCopied: Link copied to your clipboard. Paste it into Signal, Messages, or wherever you want to send it — the link is not on screen.
cameraGate.statusFailed: Your browser blocked the share and the clipboard. Tap "Show the invite" to copy it by hand.

security.title: Security
security.summaryProtected: Your secret keys on this device are wrapped with a passphrase-derived key. Unlock is required at every launch.
security.summaryUnprotected: Your secret keys are stored in plaintext on this device. Enable passphrase protection before leaving the device alone in any hostile environment.
security.passphraseHint: At least 8 characters. A four-word pass-phrase from a password manager is ideal. There is no recovery — we cannot get this back for you.
security.disableWarn: This will rewrite your wrapped secret keys as plaintext on this device. Only do this if you're sure the device is safe.
security.successEnable: Passphrase protection enabled. Write your passphrase down somewhere safe — it can't be recovered.
security.successChange: Passphrase changed. Use the new one next time you unlock.
security.successDisable: Passphrase protection disabled. Secret keys are stored in plaintext on this device again.
security.errorMismatchNew: The two new passphrases didn't match.

passkey.title: Passkey
passkey.intro: Unlock this device with your fingerprint, face, or device PIN instead of typing the passphrase. Your passphrase stays on as the fallback that always works.
passkey.enableFirst: Turn on passphrase protection first — a passkey is an extra way to unlock, never the only one.
passkey.addedOn: A passkey unlocks this device (added {{date}}). Your passphrase also still works.
passkey.promptNote: Your device may ask you to confirm twice — once to create the passkey and once to prepare it for unlocking. That's normal.
passkey.needUnlock: Unlock this device first to change passkey settings.
passkey.prfUnsupported: This device created a passkey but it can't power unlocking here (its authenticator lacks the required PRF feature). Nothing was changed — you can delete the unused passkey from your device's password manager.
passkey.failed: The passkey couldn't be set up. Nothing was changed — your passphrase still works.
passkey.successAdd: Passkey added. Next time this device locks, one tap unlocks it — and your passphrase always works too.
passkey.successRemove: Passkey removed from unlocking this device. You can also delete it from your device's password manager.

emergency.title: Emergency
emergency.intro: Two panic options for when a device is at risk. Neither contacts a server; both happen entirely on this device.
emergency.softBullet: Soft purge — strips every identifying text field (names, descriptions, areas, skills) while keeping the signed exchange ledger and your keypair. Useful if a device will briefly be handled by a hostile party.
emergency.hardBullet: Hard purge — wipes every table including private keys, rotates to a fresh node identity. Unrecoverable. The page will reload afterward.
emergency.federatedNote: Honesty note: both options act on THIS DEVICE only. Anything that already synced with your community — posts, exchanges, vouches, votes, and other signed records — persists on the community node and on other members' devices. A public vote you can no longer stand behind can be re-cast instead: the newest version is what counts.
emergency.softConfirmDescription: Identifying text on every member and post will be blanked out. Signed exchange records and your keypair will be preserved. This is not reversible.
emergency.hardConfirmDescription: Every table will be wiped — including your private keys. A fresh node identity will be generated and the app will reload. There is no undo.

help.title: Common questions
help.subtitle: Quick answers for the things members hit while using the app. Looking for the bigger picture? Check the member guide or revisit the welcome from your profile.
help.footer: Didn't find your question? Most of what isn't here is a community conversation, not an app feature — talk to a member you trust.
help.noMatches: Try different words — or clear the filter and browse by section.

startCommunity.title: Start a new community
startCommunity.subtitle: The whole path from this community's server to your own — no GitHub, no app store, no permission needed.
startCommunity.toDownloads: The downloads live on the Community infrastructure page

disputes.title: Disputes
disputes.subtitle: Exchanges someone has flagged for the community to review. Anyone can see this list — there are no admins. The two parties already see the flag on the post itself; this is where it surfaces to everyone else.
disputes.empty: Nothing flagged right now. When someone flags an exchange, it shows here so the community can see and respond.
disputes.footer: Each dispute also shows up in Decisions, where the community can vote on how to respond. Resolution closes the dispute in both places.

entry.title: Community disputes
entry.descriptionNone: No exchanges are flagged right now.
entry.descriptionOne: {{count}} exchange has been flagged for community review.
entry.descriptionOther: {{count}} exchanges have been flagged for community review.

proposals.title: Proposals
proposals.subtitle: Community decisions in progress. Anyone can start one; anyone can record an outcome once the community has agreed. Voting in-app comes later — for now this surface tracks the decisions you made elsewhere.
proposals.contested: Recorded as passed, but a standing block exists in the community-wide votes — this outcome is contested. Talk it through; the record stays.

proposals.footer: Ballots here are open: your vote is attributed, visible inside the community, and syncs to every member's device. You can change it while the proposal is open — the newest version is what counts. When the community reaches its decision, any member can record the outcome, and it takes effect everywhere.

installedArrival.title: The app is on your home screen
installedArrival.bringTitle: I already use Understoria in this phone's browser
installedArrival.bringBody: The installed app starts with its own empty storage, so it can't see your browser sign-in. Bring your identity over — two taps, no typing, under a minute.
installedArrival.newTitle: I'm new — set me up fresh
installedArrival.newBody: Take the short tour, then create your identity.

timebank.title: This is a timebank
timebank.body: Help is measured in hours. One hour of your help is worth one hour of anyone's.
timebank.bodyMore: No prices. No ratings. Every kind of help counts the same.

credit.title: You start with credit
credit.body: Everyone begins with 5 hours. You can ask for help before you give any.
credit.bodyMore: Asking is normal. It's how neighbors help each other.

identity.title: You hold your own key
identity.body: No email, no password. You have a private key that only you hold.
identity.bodyMore: No company owns it, so no one can lock you out. The other side of that trade: there's no reset and no company that can restore it — if this device is lost without a paired backup device, the identity is gone.

community.title: The community decides
community.body: There are no bosses here. People make choices together.
community.bodyMore: If something goes wrong, it starts with talking, not banning.

projects.title: Some help needs a group
projects.body: Big efforts, like a tool library, take more than one favor. A project is a shared goal many people work on.
projects.bodyMore: Look for one already going before you start a new one.

profileSetup.title: A little about you
profileSetup.intro: Pick the name neighbors will see. Everything else is optional — it helps others find you when they need someone nearby or with a particular skill. You can change any of it later in Profile.

inviteOnly.body2: If a member sent you an invitation link, follow that link instead. If not, ask a member to send one.

drills.title: Disaster drills
drills.intro: Practice the two recoveries before you need them. These checklists live only on this device — the linked runbooks in the repository stay the source of truth.

stormHub.title: Storm-hub drill
stormHub.body: Proves the go-bag server can carry the community through an internet outage.
stormHub.step1: Cut the internet: unplug the router's uplink (WAN).
stormHub.step2: Join the hub's WiFi with two members' phones.
stormHub.step3: Post from one phone and watch it appear on the other.
stormHub.step4: Bring a phone that has never seen the app: open the community's address on the hub WiFi, install, and join with an invite QR.
stormHub.step5: Check the caveats: the hub's certificate was renewed within the last 90 days, phones accept the hub's DNS, and the hub's clock is right.
stormHub.step6: Reconnect the internet and confirm the hub syncs back to the other servers.

reseed.title: Re-seed drill
reseed.body: Proves the community can be restored onto a brand-new server from one member's device, even if every server were lost.
reseed.step1: Stand up a fresh, empty server with the community's exact founder keys (operator-guide §6, "Recovering from total node loss").
reseed.step2: Set its recovery settings: RESEED_GRACE_UNTIL, and TRUSTED_SYSTEM_KEYS from the key record this app captured.
reseed.step3: From one member's device: Settings → "Restore this community onto a node", pointed at the new address.
reseed.step4: Watch the restore finish, then pull from a second member's device and check the history is all there.
reseed.step5: Turn the read gate back on (READ_AUTH=on) and confirm members still get in with their receipts alone.
reseed.step6: Remove the recovery settings from the server.
